examples: May no fate willfully misunderstand me and half grant what I wish and snatch me away not to return.
Perhaps willfully, it may be easier to think about such lengthy timescales than about the more immediate future.
And like many techy terms, it is willfully misspelled.
Yet the U.S. is willfully cutting itself off from competing in this new market.
Viacom sought to revise the ruling, insisting that YouTube was "willfully blind" of the activity.
